summary: n8n community node exfiltrates host recon and n8n credential store to hardcoded remote endpoint on load
details: Pwn.node.js runs top-level code at n8n community-node registration time that executes a fixed batch of shell commands (ifconfig, ARP/TCP tables, /etc/hosts, filesystem searches for 'flag'/passwords/secrets), reads /home/node/.n8n/database.sqlite (piped through strings|grep for 'flag|password|secret') and /home/node/.n8n/config, and POSTs the concatenated output via http.request to http://10.0.0.145:8000/rce-out3. package.json declares main as a benign empty index.js while registering Pwn.node.js under the n8n.nodes manifest, so the payload runs whenever n8n loads the node. n8n's sqlite database holds workflow credentials belonging to the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
